issues: The draft short description incorrectly calls this a 'Centennial Cup tier two best-of-seven series' — the OCR identifies it as the Doyle Cup (Alberta vs BC), not the Centennial Cup. Actually, re-reading the OCR, it does say 'Centennial Cup tier two junior hockey elimination series' — this appears to be the correct terminology used in the article. However, the draft omits key context about Red Deer's path to the series (they finished fourth in the Alberta Junior League and beat Calgary Spurs in 7, Calgary Canucks in 7, and St. Albert Saints in 6). The draft also incorrectly states the Sockeyes 'extended their playoff winning streak to 15 games with their fourth consecutive series sweep' — the OCR says they PUT their 15-game streak ON THE LINE (i.e., the streak is already at 15 going in, the series against Red Deer has not yet been played). The description implies the streak was extended by the Mowatt Cup win, conflating future and past events.

The draft says the Sockeyes 'recently defeated Quesnel Millionaires 5-1 to win the Mowatt Cup in two straight games, extending their playoff winning streak to 15 games' — the OCR makes clear the 15-game streak is what they are putting on the line in the upcoming Red Deer series, not something extended by the Quesnel win. The phrasing in the draft is misleading.
The draft omits Red Deer's Alberta playoff path (beat Calgary Spurs in 7, Calgary Canucks in 7, St. Albert Saints in 6), which is relevant context in the article.
Game time details (7:30 p.m. Tuesday and Wednesday at Richmond Arena, remainder in Red Deer) are omitted from the long description.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes will put their 15-game playoff winning streak on the line Tuesday against Alberta champion Red Deer Rustlers in the opening game of a best-of-seven Centennial Cup tier-two elimination series at Richmond Arena.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Richmond Sockeyes will put their 15-game playoff winning streak on the line Tuesday at 7:30 p.m. when they host Alberta champion Red Deer Rustlers in Game 1 of the best-of-seven Centennial Cup tier-two junior hockey elimination series at Richmond Arena. The Sockeyes earned their spot with a 5-1 win over Quesnel Millionaires to take the Mowatt Cup in two straight — their fourth consecutive series sweep. Red Deer, who finished fourth in the Alberta Junior League, advanced by defeating the Calgary Spurs in seven games, Calgary Canucks in seven, and St. Albert Saints in six. Games 1 and 2 are in Richmond; the remainder of the series shifts to Red Deer.
review.notes: The headline is accurate per the OCR. The main issues were misleading phrasing around the 15-game streak (it is being put on the line, not extended, in the upcoming series) and omission of Red Deer's Alberta playoff path and game schedule details. Note also that the article spells the trophy "Mowatt Cup" — the standard archive spelling is "Mowat Cup"; editor may wish to standardize.
